Detained ~30 hours. ACLU/UM suit; city settled $300,000 (2024) with landmark policy reforms restricting FRT-only warrants/lineups.

Police_Department: Fargo Police Department; U.S. Marshals Service (arrest)
Technology: AI facial recognition (bank CCTV → Fargo PD investigation)
Alert_Reason: Organized bank fraud / identity theft in Fargo (Apr-May 2025)
Underlying_Error: AI Facial Recognition False Match; Officer Failed to Verify
Narrative_Summary: 50-year-old Tennessee grandmother arrested at gunpoint by U.S. Marshals on July 14, 2025, while babysitting four children, on Fargo ND felony warrants after investigators used facial recognition on bank surveillance of a woman using a fake military ID. She had never been to North Dakota. Held without bail in Tennessee ~4 months, extradited to Cass County jail, then released Christmas Eve 2025 after attorney Jay Greenwood produced bank records proving she was >1,200 miles away in Tennessee. Charges dismissed (without prejudice). Lost home, car, and dog while incarcerated. Fargo PD later changed FRT policy.
Current_Case_Status: Charges dismissed without prejudice; FRT policy under revision
Agency_Response: Fargo Chief Zibolski defended investigation aspects; said case remains active regarding the fraud ring; issued temporary FRT directive / policy change (Mar 2026).
Policy_Changes: Fargo PD restricted/reviewed facial recognition use after the case publicly broke.
Notes: Some reporting mentions Clearview AI / West Fargo role; primary Fargo investigation attribution varies — Vendor left NULL unless court docs confirm. Fox cited probation hold nuance; still FRT-linked extradition detention.

Underlying_Error: OCR Misread; Database Error; Human Data Entry; Officer Failed to Verify
Narrative_Summary: Auto writer Joel Feder and his wife were boxed in by four Plymouth PD squad cars at a Kohl's lot after Flock ALPR alerts on their Range Rover press loaner. Officers had tracked the car for days via Flock. Cause: a lost NJ manufacturer plate reported in California was entered into NCIC incomplete as 34 DTM (missing small middle digits); Feder's plate was 34 10 DTM, and Flock OCR also missed those characters. VIN clean; plate was lost not stolen; ~1 hour detention; hands on guns.
Agency_Response: Plymouth PD: incomplete NCIC entry from California report; Flock alerts on June 26 and June 28, 2026.
Vendor_Response: Flock: reviewing incident; alert should be one part of an investigation, not the whole basis for a stop (per CBS Minnesota).
Notes: Primary: Feder first-person (The Drive). Local TV corroboration: FOX 9 and CBS Minnesota/WCCO. Do not cite Infowars/Alex Jones secondary amplification.
